# Resultant Force Calculation

## Short Teaching Summary
When two forces act in the same direction, the resultant force is the sum of the individual forces.

## Difficulty
0.00

## Prerequisites
- None recorded.

## Related Concepts
- Force causes acceleration (example_of)

## Misconceptions
- None recorded.

## Best Examples
- When two forces act in the same direction, the resultant force is larger than either individual force, and it is calculated by adding their sizes together.

## Best Analogy
- None recorded.
